subject: LU-5519 lfsck: repair bad name hash for striped directory

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/26603/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/26603/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This series contains a few instances of the following:

 
char *name2 = lfsck_env_info(env)->lti_tmpbuf2;
 
rc = snprintf(name2, LFSCK_TMPBUF_LEN - 1, DFID":%u",
                      PFID(fid), index);
name2[rc] = '\0';

There are a few things wrong here:

1) Please sizeof(lfsck_env_info(env)->lti_tmpbuf2), ...) rather than LFSCK_TMPBUF_LEN - 1.

2) There is no need for the -1. Pass the size of the buffer to snprintf().

3) If the buffer size is positive then snprintf() will add a terminal '\0' regardless of whether or not the string fit in the buffer.

4) snpritnf() returns the number of characters that *would* have been written if the buffer was large enough. So if the buffer was too small then setting name2[rc] = '\0' is an out of bounds array access.

Please use rc = snprintf(name2, sizeof(...), ...) and skip the manual '\0' termination.

Patch Set 12:

> how is it different from the current approach? you ask remote node to rescan. I don't see a strong reason to do this "right away". in the end, this is remote LFSCK who'll be doing scanning. so why to invent some new mechanism? as for async/sync mode - we do have ->do_object_sync() which (being implemented in OUT) could be used for this purpose.

The issue of handling setting LMV EA remotely via OUT RPC has been done in the new version set 12

parents.subject: LU-5584 llite: ensure all data flush out when umount

commit.subject: LU-5519 lfsck: repair bad name hash for striped directory
commit.message:
LU-5519 lfsck: repair bad name hash for striped directory

If the name hash of some name entry under the striped directory
does not match the shard of the striped directory, then the LFSCK
will repair the inconsistency. Ideally, the LFSCK should migrate
the name entry from the current MDT to the right MDT (another one),
but before the async commit finished, the LFSCK will change the
striped directory's hash type as LMV_HASH_TYPE_UNKNOWN and mark
the lmv flags as LMV_HASH_FLAG_BAD_TYPE.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I665a77473be1fead09c12cb7fb5d0ffa739ffe87
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/11846
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
